concerns.text: FR-002 requires '≥80% confidence threshold' for classification heuristics. The spec does not define the metric or procedure to calculate this 'confidence' (e.g., keyword match count, probability score, or external validator). Without a defined calculation method, this requirement is unverifiable.

FR-007 mandates sensitivity analysis sweeping values across '{0.01, 0.05, 0.1}'. The spec fails to identify WHICH decision thresholds these values apply to (e.g., p-value cutoff, sentiment score cutoff, or comment depth). A test cannot verify the sweep without knowing the target variable.

FR-012 requires analysis to complete 'within 6 hours'. The spec does not define the input dataset size (e.g., number of PRs or GB of data) against which this time limit is measured. Performance on 100 PRs is trivial; on 1M PRs it may be impossible. The success criterion is unmeasurable without a load profile.

SC-001 states distribution is measured 'to detect differences'. This describes an intent, not a measurable outcome. A success criterion must define the observable result (e.g., 'p-value < 0.05' or 'effect size > 0.2') or the specific statistical test result required to pass.

SC-002 states sentiment score is measured 'to detect differences'. Similar to SC-001, this is an intent. It must specify the measurable condition for success (e.g., 'statistically significant difference in mean sentiment scores between groups').

SC-003 states merge latency is measured 'to detect differences'. This is an intent, not a measurable success criterion. It requires a specific threshold or statistical condition (e.g., 'median merge time difference > X hours' or 'p < 0.05') to be verifiable.
